label: Hong Kong visitor arrivals reach nearly ten million in early twenty twenty-six
label_zh: 香港訪客人數在2026年初接近一千萬

timeline.title: Hong Kong Tourism Hits 9.95 Million Visitors in Early 2026 Amid Mainland Travel Surge
timeline.title_zh: 2026 年初香港旅遊業迎來 995 萬訪客，受惠於內地旅遊激增
timeline.sub_title: Official figures from the Hong Kong Tourism Board reveal a major 18 percent increase in arrivals, driven by an extended Spring Festival and a robust recovery in long-haul international markets.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 香港旅遊發展局的官方數字顯示，入境人數大幅增加 18%，主要受惠於春節假期延長和長途國際市場的強勁復甦。
timeline.summary:
Hong Kong’s tourism sector has achieved a significant milestone in the first two months of 2026, recording 9.95 million visitor arrivals. This figure represents an 18 percent year-on-year increase compared to the same period in 2025, signaling a powerful start to the year for the city's hospitality and retail industries. The Hong Kong Tourism Board (HKTB) released provisional data on Tuesday indicating that the growth was largely propelled by a surge in visitors from mainland China, though several international markets also showed impressive gains. 
 [ref:8, 1, 2]
Mainland China remains the primary driver of Hong Kong's tourism recovery, accounting for approximately 7.89 million arrivals in January and February. This [ref:8, 11] represents a 22 percent increase from the previous year, with the HKTB attributing the influx to a record-long nine-day Spring Festival holiday and a diverse calendar of mega-events hosted throughout the city. February [ref:3] alone saw a 40 percent total increase in arrivals, with mainland visitor numbers jumping by 53 percent compared to February 2025. This [ref:8, 11] monthly spike was further supported by promotional efforts targeting high-spending overnight visitors and the city's reputation as a premier destination for traditional Lunar New Year celebrations.

Beyond the mainland, the non-mainland market recorded 2.06 million arrivals in the first two months, marking an 8 percent year-on-year rise. Long-haul markets were particularly strong, with visitor numbers from Australia, France, and Germany growing by 20 to 30 percent. However [ref:9, 3, 2, 1, 7, 8], growth in short-haul markets was more tempered, showing only a marginal 0.1 percent increase due to the fluctuating dates of Ramadan and different regional holiday schedules. Despite [ref:11] these fluctuations, the government remains optimistic, projecting a total of 53.8 million visitors for the full year of 2026, which would surpass the 49.9 million recorded in 2025.

The surge in tourism is also reflected in transportation and logistics data. The Airport Authority Hong Kong reported that passenger traffic at Hong Kong International Airport (HKIA) reached 10.93 million trips in the first two months of the year, an 11.7 percent increase. This growth [ref:8] was bolstered by a 30 percent rise in transfer and transit traffic, highlighting Hong Kong’s status as a regional aviation hub. Additionally, the city’s cruise industry continues to thrive, with 52 cruise vessels docking in the first two months, carrying over 180,000 passengers. New initiatives [ref:4], such as the relaunch of transit routes through Victoria Harbour for designated cruise ships, are expected to further enhance the city's appeal to international travelers.

To sustain this momentum, the Hong Kong government has allocated $1.66 billion to the Tourism Board in the latest budget. These funds are earmarked for scaling up flagship events, launching new light-themed festivals to replace the long-running "A Symphony of Lights," and expanding marketing efforts in emerging markets like the Middle East and ASEAN nations. As the city continues [ref:5] to diversify its offerings, officials expect the combination of infrastructure upgrades and innovative cultural attractions to keep visitor numbers on an upward trajectory throughout 2026.
timeline.summary_zh:
2026 年前兩個月，香港旅遊業已達到一個重要的里程碑，錄得 995 萬旅客入境。與 2025 年同期相比，這一數字同比增長 18%，預示著這座城市酒店業和零售業迎來了強勁的開年。香港旅遊發展局 (HKTB) 週二公布的初步數據顯示，增長主要得益於來自中國內地旅客的激增，儘管一些國際市場也顯示出顯著增長。[ref:8, 1, 2]
中國內地仍然是香港旅遊復甦的主要驅動力，1 月和 2 月的入境人數約為 789 萬。這比去年同期增長了 22%，HKTB 將此歸因於創紀錄的九天春節假期以及全市舉辦的各種大型活動。僅在 2 月份，入境總人數就增加了 40%，其中內地旅客數量比 2025 年 2 月增加了 53%。月度激增得到了針對高消費過夜旅客的推廣活動以及香港作為傳統農曆新年慶祝活動首選目的地的聲譽的支持。[ref:8, 11, 3]
此外，非內地市場在首兩個月錄得 206 萬入境人次，同比增長 8%。長途市場表現尤為強勁，來自澳大利亞、法國和德國的旅客數字增長了 20% 至 30%。然而，短途市場的增長較為平穩，由於齋戒月日期的波動和不同的地區節假日安排，僅顯示出 0.1% 的微弱增長。儘管存在這些波動，政府仍保持樂觀，預計 2026 年全年旅客總數將達到 5380 萬，將超過 2025 年記錄的 4990 萬。[ref:9, 3, 2, 1, 7, 8, 11]
旅遊業的激增也反映在交通和物流數據中。香港機場管理局報告稱，2026 年前兩個月，香港國際機場 (HKIA) 的客運量達到 1093 萬人次，增長 11.7%。轉機和過境交通量增長 30% 促進了這一增長，突顯了香港作為區域航空樞紐的地位。此外，該市的郵輪旅遊業持續蓬勃發展，首兩個月有 52 艘郵輪停靠，載客量超過 18 萬人次。新的舉措，例如重新啟動經維多利亞港的指定郵輪航線，預計將進一步提升香港對國際旅客的吸引力。[ref:8, 4]
為維持這一勢頭，香港政府在最新的預算中向旅遊局撥款 16.6 億港元。這些資金將用於擴大旗艦活動的規模，推出新的燈光主題節日以取代長期舉辦的「A Symphony of Lights」，並擴大在中東和東盟國家等新興市場的營銷力度。隨著城市不斷擴展其產品組合，官員們預計基礎設施升級和創新的文化景點將使旅客數量在 2026 年全年保持上升趨勢。[ref:5]

timeline.title: Hong Kong Tourism Hits Nearly Ten Million Visitors During the First Two Months of Twenty Twenty-Six
timeline.title_zh: 香港旅遊業在2026年前兩個月吸引近千萬訪客
timeline.sub_title: Data from the Hong Kong Tourism Board reveals an 18 percent increase in arrivals, bolstered by a surge in mainland travelers and a record-breaking nine-day Spring Festival holiday.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 香港旅遊發展局數據顯示，入境人數增加18%，受惠於內地旅客激增及創紀錄的九天春節假期。
timeline.summary:
Hong Kong’s tourism industry has recorded a powerful start to 2026, with visitor arrivals reaching 9.95 million in the first two months of the year. This figure represents an 18 percent increase compared to the same period in 2025, signaling a sustained and robust recovery for the city’s travel sector. The Hong Kong Tourism Board released these provisional figures on Tuesday, highlighting that the growth was largely fueled by a significant influx of travelers from the Chinese mainland, which remains the city’s largest source market.

According to the official data, mainland Chinese visitors accounted for approximately 7.9 million arrivals, marking a 22 percent year-on-year rise. The surge [ref:1, 4] was particularly pronounced in February, which saw total arrivals reach 5.14 million—a 40 percent jump from the previous year. This spike [ref:5] was attributed to the record-breaking nine-day Spring Festival holiday on the mainland, the longest on record, which encouraged many families to travel across the border. During the month of February alone, mainland arrivals surged by 53 percent to over 4.2 million, as visitors flocked to the city for various festive celebrations and mega-events.

Beyond the mainland market, Hong Kong also saw positive momentum in international travel. Visitor arrivals [ref:5] from non-mainland markets totaled 2.06 million in the first two months, an 8 percent increase over 2025. Long-haul markets [ref:1, 7, 5] showed particularly impressive results, with arrivals from countries such as Australia, France, and Germany growing by 20 to 30 percent. While short [ref:4, 1, 7, 5]-haul markets outside of mainland China saw more marginal growth due to shifting holiday calendars and the timing of Ramadan, the overall trend points toward a diversifying visitor base.

The cruise industry has played a significant role in this early-year success. Reports from the Culture, Sports and Tourism Bureau indicate that 52 cruise vessels docked in Hong Kong during January and February, carrying more than 180,000 passengers. A notable highlight [ref:2] was the relaunch of the newly refurbished Celebrity Solstice, which became the first vessel to utilize a new transit arrangement allowing designated ships to pass through Victoria Harbour between 6 p.m. and 6 a.m. without prior approval. This initiative is part of a broader effort to enhance the scenic experience for cruise passengers and boost the city’s status as a regional cruise hub.

Looking ahead, government officials remain optimistic about the city's tourism trajectory. Secretary for Culture, Sports and Tourism Rosanna Law Shuk-pui recently projected that Hong Kong will welcome a total of 53.8 million visitors by the end of 2026, which would represent an 8 percent increase over the 49.9 million arrivals recorded in 2025. To support this growth [ref:6], the government and the Tourism Board are shifting their focus toward experiential tourism, encouraging the industry to develop unique itineraries that highlight the city’s cultural heritage, natural landscapes, and diverse culinary scene. With the full operation of the three-runway system at the airport and a packed calendar of international events, the city is well-positioned to maintain its momentum throughout the remainder of the year.
timeline.summary_zh:
香港旅遊業在2026年初迎來強勁開局，首兩個月訪港旅客達995萬人次。數字較2025年同期增長18%，顯示本港旅遊業持續穩健復甦。香港旅遊發展局周二公布初步數字，強調增長主要由內地旅客顯著增加所帶動，內地仍是本港最大的客源市場。
官方數據顯示，內地訪港旅客約790萬人次，按年增長22%。增幅尤以2月為甚，該月總入境人數達514萬人次，較去年同期躍升40%。此增長歸因於內地創紀錄的九天春節假期，鼓勵了許多家庭跨境旅遊。僅在2月份，內地旅客就激增53%至超過420萬人次，因旅客湧入本港參與各種節慶慶典和大型活動。[ref:1, 4, 5]
除了內地市場，香港國際旅遊業亦見增長。非內地市場的旅客首兩個月錄得206萬人次，較2025年增長8%。長途市場表現尤其亮眼，來自澳洲、法國和德國等國家的旅客增長20%至30%。儘管內地以外的短途市場因假期錯配和齋戒月時間影響，增長較為溫和，但整體趨勢顯示客源基礎日益多元化。[ref:5, 1, 7, 4]
郵輪業在本年初的成功中扮演了重要角色。文化、體育及旅遊局的報告指出，1月和2月共有52艘郵輪停泊香港，載客量超過18萬人次。其中一個亮點是新翻修的Celebrity Solstice郵輪重新啟航，成為首艘利用新過境安排的船隻，允許指定郵輪在未經事先批准的情況下，於晚上6時至早上6時之間通過維多利亞港。這項舉措旨在提升郵輪旅客的觀光體驗，並鞏固香港作為區域郵輪樞紐的地位。[ref:2]
展望未來，政府官員對本港旅遊業的發展前景保持樂觀。文化、體育及旅遊局局長楊潤雄最近預計，香港在2026年底將接待總計5,380萬名旅客，較2025年的4,990萬人次增長8%。為支持此增長，政府和旅發局正將重點轉向體驗式旅遊，鼓勵業界開發突顯本港文化遺產、自然風光和多元美食的獨特行程。隨著機場三跑道系統全面運作及國際盛事日程緊湊，本港已準備就緒，有望保持全年增長勢頭。[ref:6]
